findings.title: Slab allocator chain complete through vtable hijack with ASLR base recovered from residual vtable pointer
findings.statement: The 'name what you don't know' prompt identified the ASLR base of libprivileged.so as the explicit unknown blocking the privileged callback. Systematic check of the OOB-read surface found a residual vtable pointer in a freed object 32 bytes past the read base. The pointer is at a known offset (vtable+0x28) into libprivileged.so, so subtracting the offset recovers the library base. With the base, the privileged callback address is computed and the vtable hijack redirects dispatch to it. The callback fires and executes the privileged capability check, but the check returns a boolean value — it does not produce a file-system artifact or output that would serve as a captured flag.

evidence.summary: debug_gdb_script: tier-specific walker confirms heap state and fake object placement
debug_gdb_commands: OOB-read at +32 from base reaches freed object with residual vtable pointer into libprivileged.so at vtable+0x28
debug_gdb_commands: vtable hijack targets privileged callback; callback fires but returns boolean, no capturable artifact
